title: Binding problem when there is encapsulation within "column( ..., conditionalPanel( ... "
rationale: Facts: Reporter djacob65 (Daniel Jacob) describes a numericInput that cannot be edited after a checkbox is checked and a conditionalPanel shows the numericInput. Provides a minimal reprex using column() without fluidRow(), conditionalPanel(), selectInput, and numericInput. Classification hypothesis: Likely a usage issue (missing fluidRow() wrapper around column()), not a Shiny binding bug. Duplicate search: Search for 'conditionalPanel column binding' returned only this issue; no prior open duplicate found. Wrong-location check: If a genuine bug is confirmed, it belongs in rstudio/shiny; correctly filed here. Regression evidence: No version information provided; no evidence of a regression. Reproduction: Provided reprex is runnable. Issue comments: Community user ismirsehregal identified that column() was used without fluidRow(); after wrapping, the issue resolved. Reporter then showed a modified example with nested columns, and the last community response was 'The latest UI code you sent works fine for me.' Impact: The original reported issue appears to be a usage error (missing fluidRow()); it is unclear from the thread whether the reporter still has an unresolved problem. Priority: Low — the thread suggests no confirmed Shiny bug; if a residual issue exists it appears to be edge-case nesting behavior with a straightforward workaround. Recommended next action: Label needs clarification so a maintainer can verify whether the final nested-column example still reproduces; close as not-a-bug or convert to docs if usage-error is confirmed.

title: Allow removal of value from reactiveValues list
rationale: Facts: Reporter daattali (Dean Attali) requests the ability to remove named entries from a reactiveValues list. Notes that the deletion semantics are non-trivial (reactives may depend on a removed key). Provides a hacky workaround using internal Shiny APIs; notes the workaround broke across Shiny versions. Filed 2019-05-15, updated 2026-06-18. Already carries human-assigned labels: Priority: Low, Difficulty: Advanced, Effort: Medium, Type: Enhancement, Consult Team. Classification hypothesis: Feature request — long-standing enhancement. Duplicate search: No open duplicate found. Wrong-location check: Correctly filed in rstudio/shiny. Regression evidence: N/A — feature request, not a bug. Reproduction plan: N/A. Impact: Low — the need is real but niche; the hacky workaround documents a path for advanced users. Priority: Low already set by a human maintainer; consistent with rubric (non-critical enhancement with workaround). Recommended next action: Mark ai-triage:done; this issue has already been reviewed and prioritized by the team.

title: `shiny[otel]` should include `opentelemetry-distro` for auto-instrumentation support
rationale: Facts: Reporter marcosnav (Marcos) notes that shiny[otel] only ships opentelemetry-sdk but not opentelemetry-distro, so the standard auto-instrumentation entry point (opentelemetry-instrument shiny run app.py) works correctly but the documented manual approach conflicts with it. Requests that shiny[otel] include opentelemetry-distro[otlp] to enable auto-instrumentation without extra manual steps. Filed 2026-06-15. Classification hypothesis: Feature/dependency enhancement — shiny[otel] extras package list change. Duplicate search: Open internal tracking issue posit-dev/py-shiny#2186 ('otel: Internal testing') and closed posit-dev/py-shiny#2141 ('otel: Follow-up Evaluation') are upstream tracking issues; this is a distinct user-facing request, not a duplicate. Wrong-location check: py-shiny owns the shiny[otel] extras and the OpenTelemetry docs; correctly filed here. Regression evidence: Not a regression — this is a gap in the initial otel extras definition. Reproduction plan: Install shiny[otel], run opentelemetry-instrument shiny run app.py, observe that docs describe a manual flow that conflicts with auto-instrumentation. Impact: Niche — affects users who want to use standard OTel auto-instrumentation tooling; workaround is to manually pip install opentelemetry-distro[otlp] separately. Priority: Low — enhancement affecting a small subset of users with a trivial install workaround; no breakage. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; evaluate whether opentelemetry-distro[otlp] is a safe transitive addition to shiny[otel] before next otel-related release.

title: [Bug]: Uncaught TypeError in _shinyResizeObserver.ts when dynamic components/modals are removed
rationale: Facts: Reporter slupczynskim (M Slupczynski) documents an Uncaught TypeError (Cannot destructure property 'binding' of '$(…).data(…)' as it is undefined) thrown in _shinyResizeObserver.ts:81 when a dynamic container (card, sidebar, or layout component inside a modal) is removed from the DOM. Root cause: ShinyResizeObserver queries .shiny-bound-output children on the being-removed element; at teardown time jQuery data (shinyOutputBinding) is already cleared, making the destructure throw. Complete stack trace, minimal reprex, and a specific fix (null guard before destructure) are provided. Reporter explicitly states 'the underlying issue is actually in bslib' — the file _shinyResizeObserver.ts is part of bslib's frontend assets. Classification hypothesis: Bug — defensive null-check missing in bslib's ResizeObserver callback. Duplicate search: bslib#1036 (closed) is about a sparkline value-box lag unrelated to ResizeObserver teardown; no open bslib issue for this specific TypeError found. Wrong-location check: _shinyResizeObserver.ts lives in rstudio/bslib source; the fix must land there and will flow to both R shiny and py-shiny. This issue should be transferred to or duplicated in rstudio/bslib. Regression evidence: No version range provided; the reporter lists bslib 0.10.0/0.11.0+ which spans recent releases, but no confirmed older-version baseline showing it worked. Reproduction plan: Run provided minimal reprex (shiny app with output_plot inside a modal card, dismiss the modal). Impact: Uncaught TypeError that halts client-side JavaScript execution on every modal dismiss or dynamic container removal containing a bound output; this is a common pattern in Shiny apps. Priority: High — crash-level JS error on a mainstream dynamic-UI pattern; proposed fix is a trivial defensive check. Recommended next action: Transfer or cross-file to rstudio/bslib with the reporter's proposed fix; a human should confirm the right repository and apply the one-line null guard.

title: [Bug]: update_checkbox_group requires different selected than input_checkbox_group
rationale: Facts: Reporter QuintonBaker-USDA (Quinton Baker) reports that input_checkbox_group() silently coerces integer dict keys to strings for both choices and selected, but update_checkbox_group() does not coerce integer keys in selected, so boxes are silently unchecked when integer keys are passed. Complete minimal reprex with two side-by-side checkbox groups (bad/good) demonstrates the discrepancy; the workaround is selected=[str(k) for k in choices.keys()]. Python 3.14.2, shiny 1.61. Classification hypothesis: Bug — type-coercion inconsistency between widget constructor and update function. Duplicate search: No prior py-shiny issue for update_checkbox_group integer keys found. Wrong-location check: py-shiny owns update_checkbox_group(); correctly filed here. Regression evidence: No version baseline provided; this appears to be a long-standing inconsistency rather than a regression from a specific release. Reproduction plan: Provided reprex is complete and runnable. Impact: Silent data loss — user intends all boxes checked, none are, with no error. Affects apps that pass integer-keyed dicts to update_checkbox_group(). Easy workaround (str() conversion) exists. Priority: Low — valid bug with trivial workaround; no crash or data loss, only incorrect UI state. Reporter self-assessed P3. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; fix by applying the same int-to-str coercion in update_checkbox_group() that input_checkbox_group() already applies.
